title: Perfectly Pan Seared Saudi Arabian Lamb Kebabs
description: A traditional Saudi Arabian dish featuring lamb kebabs, served with a unique twist of Christmas pudding and sashimi-cut lamb, perfect for a solo dinner, this recipe is low-calorie-friendly and packed with flavor, with a grassy and flaky texture from the lamb and pudding.
instructions: Preheat a large skillet over medium-high heat.
In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper.
Add the lamb cubes to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Thread the marinated lamb cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Add the skewers to the preheated skillet and cook for 5 minutes per side, or until the lamb is cooked to an internal temperature of 145 degrees.
While the lamb is cooking, heat the Christmas pudding in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring constantly.
Once the lamb is cooked, serve with the warmed Christmas pudding and garnish with fresh parsley.
Slice the lamb into thin strips, similar to sashimi, and serve immediately.
